message:
feat(backend-cpu): replace sequential interpreter with dependency-wave parallel executor

Rewrite the CPU backend execution engine from a thin eval_jaxpr wrapper
to a dependency-aware parallel scheduler that evaluates independent
equations concurrently using rayon.

Scheduling algorithm:
- Build an environment map from input variables to their values
- Iterate in waves: collect all equations whose inputs are ready and
  that precede the next effect barrier (sub_jaxprs or side effects)
- If multiple equations are ready and rayon has >1 thread, evaluate
  them in parallel via par_iter, then merge results back sequentially
- Effect-bearing equations are evaluated as serial barriers to preserve
  ordering guarantees
- Track max_ready_wave width for observability

Helper functions:
- equation_inputs_ready(): check if all Atom::Var inputs are in env
- resolve_equation_inputs(): extract Vec<Value> from env for one eqn
- evaluate_equation(): single-equation eval via fj_lax::eval_primitive
- evaluate_jaxpr_parallel_inner(): core scheduling loop with wave stats
- first_missing_input_var(): diagnostics for missing variable errors

Dependencies:
- Add rayon 1.11 and fj-lax crate dependency to fj-backend-cpu

Benchmarks (backend_baseline.rs):
- Add make_wide_parallel_jaxpr(width) helper that builds a fan-out +
  reduction tree of width independent Add operations
- Add bench_execute_wide_parallel (64-wide, backend) and
  bench_interpreter_wide_parallel (64-wide, interpreter) for A/B
  comparison between parallel backend and sequential interpreter

Tests:
- test_parallel_independent_ops: verifies 2 independent Neg ops are
  scheduled in a wave of width >= 2
- test_parallel_correctness: backend output matches interpreter output
- test_parallel_no_data_race: 8 threads x 64 iterations concurrent
  execution with value correctness assertions

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
